requirement.original: Fix the write-guard sentinel integration (Task B, second attempt). QA FAILURE 1: sentinel writer keys by session_id (CLAUDE_TASK_ID:-SID), write-guard reads by data.task_id — keys never match, sentinel never found. QA FAILURE 2: targetless /allow Write grants (no 'target' field) trigger CF-1 denial for all write operations, breaking backward compatibility. Both fixes must be backward-compatible and pass the existing test suite.
requirement.clarified: The working tree already contains uncommitted fixes for both bugs (candidate-list in write-guard + wildcard in match_sentinel_grant_for_write). Dev must verify the fixes are correct, add the missing targetless-wildcard test case, ensure all tests pass, and commit only the four in-scope hook files.
requirement.what: Complete and commit the two bug fixes for write-guard sentinel integration: (1) candidate-list task_id resolution in write-guard and posttool, (2) wildcard predicate for targetless grants in match_sentinel_grant_for_write, plus test coverage for both
requirement.why: QA rejected prior implementation because the sentinel was silently never found (task_id mismatch) and targetless /allow Write grants regressed to CF-1 denial

included: allowlist.py: verify wildcard predicate in match_sentinel_grant_for_write (working tree line 521-523)
pretool-write-guard.sh: verify candidate-list task_id resolution (working tree lines 59-65)
posttool-allowlist-consume.py: verify mirrored candidate-list for Write consumption (working tree lines 148-155)
excluded: hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (sibling task 20260521-090200 changes — must NOT be committed in this task)
/allow Write /specific/path target-specific grant semantics (args_contain vs target field issue — out of scope per WN1)

success_criteria: match_sentinel_grant_for_write({op:'Write'} no target field, session match) returns entry not None (wildcard)
write-guard exits 0 when sentinel keyed by session_id and CLAUDE_TASK_ID is unset
All 40 pre-existing tests continue to pass (no regressions)
git diff HEAD for this task shows ONLY the four in-scope files (not userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh)
constraints: Wildcard predicate: entry_target is None or entry_target == target_path (NOT just entry_target == target_path)
Candidate-list priority order: [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id] where writer_key = env_task_id if env_task_id else session_id
Candidate-list must deduplicate (seen set) to avoid double-loading when writer_key == session_id
hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh MUST NOT appear in this task's git diff
Do NOT use 'target_path' as field name — the schema field is 'target'

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (sentinel writer — read for context, must not be modified in this task)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: For /allow Write /specific/path, the sentinel writer produces {op:'Write', args_contain:['/specific/path']} (no 'target' field). match_sentinel_grant_for_write() checks 'target' not 'args_contain', so the target-path entry falls through to the wildcard condition (entry_target is None -> match any path). Net effect: /allow Write /path grants are treated as wildcard, less strict than intended. Not a regression from committed code; not one of the two QA-identified critical bugs. Deferred to a future cycle.

AC3 Bash-with-Write-sentinel test has softened assertion (QA finding: does not assert sentinel survives Bash event). Should be strengthened in a future cycle to assert os.path.exists(sentinel_path) is True after Bash posttool runs with a Write-targeted sentinel. Noted but deprioritized — AC3 core cases (Write success/failure consume) pass.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Write-guard sentinel bypass silently fails in realistic usage; targetless /allow Write grants block all overwrites
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Two independent L4 logic defects in the committed code: (1) task_id resolution uses only data.task_id, never trying session_id as the sentinel file key when CLAUDE_TASK_ID is unset; (2) match_sentinel_grant_for_write() (added in prior attempt) lacks wildcard predicate for absent 'target' field
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: aea75d9 (chore: ship cycle 20260522-000000) — committed the prior cycle's implementation with both defects
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Prior cycle implemented the structural plumbing but the task_id resolution was a read-side fix only (data.task_id), not aligned with the writer's key logic. The wildcard predicate was simply absent from the implementation.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Sentinel lookup silently returns None for every task where CLAUDE_TASK_ID is unset (common case), leaving the sentinel bypass unreachable. CF-1 firing for targetless grants breaks all existing /allow Write users.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: QA report date: 2026-05-24. Both bugs confirmed via QA codex adversarial review + live subprocess testing (QA report qa-report-20260522-080646-B.json section_6).
observations: sentinel writer userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:183: TASK_ID='${CLAUDE_TASK_ID:-${SID}}' (observed: Read in this session)
working-tree allowlist.py:521-523: entry_target is None or entry_target == target_path (wildcard) (observed: Read in this session)
working-tree pretool-write-guard.sh:59-65: candidate-list [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id] (observed: Read in this session)
live test: candidate-list finds sentinel at session_id when CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset and data_task_id differs (observed: subprocess test in this session)
40/40 tests pass on working tree (observed: pytest run in this session)
no test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path in TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite (observed: grep + Read in this session)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete (both inferred from code and observed via live test)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: QA report section_6 (confirmed via live subprocess test): sentinel writer keyed by SID, write-guard reads data.task_id -> file not found -> sentinel miss. Code trace: {op:'Write'} no target field -> entry_target = None -> None == target_path -> False -> returns None -> valid_no_match -> CF-1.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — regression identified by QA, not user-reported regression with bisect trigger

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: task_id key mismatch: sentinel writer writes keyed by session_id ... sentinel reader looks up by data.task_id. In practice these keys never match, so sentinels are never found and the grant mechanism silently fails.
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: write-guard resolves sentinel key via candidate-list [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id]; finds sentinel when CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset and sentinel keyed by session_id

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: grants with no 'target' field (targetless, meaning 'allow any write') were previously allowed. After the Task B implementation, these targetless grants now trigger CF-1 denial for all write operations
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: match_sentinel_grant_for_write returns entry (not None) when op=='Write' and target field is absent; new test_targetless_wildcard_matches_any_path passes

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Both fixes must be backward-compatible and pass the existing test suite.
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: All 40 pre-existing tests pass; no new regressions; syntax validation passes for all modified files

novelty_check.rationale: Prior attempt had incorrect task_id resolution (data.task_id only, missing writer-aligned fallback to session_id) and missing wildcard predicate. This attempt uses a different resolution strategy (candidate-list mirroring writer priority) and adds the wildcard. These are two distinct corrections at L4; the prior attempt made changes at L4 but incomplete ones — this attempt targets the specific gaps the prior attempt missed.
